targets.label: Min 2026 (Jo Bhakdi)
targets.note: If not reached by Dec 2026, market is disappointed.

targets.label: Forced re-rating (Jo Bhakdi)
targets.note: Forcing function for a forced market re-rating.

news.title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Fleet Reaches 10 Model Y Vehicles
news.summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet hit a record of 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles operating driverless.
news.impact_reason: Direct evidence of modest growth in Austin's unsupervised fleet size enhances scaling momentum by increasing real-world miles and data collection for further unsupervised expansion.

news.title: FSD v14.3+ Ready for Unsupervised Robotaxi Operations
news.summary: Industry analysts indicate Tesla has FSD versions 14.3.2.1 or 14.4 ready for unsupervised robotaxi deployment, removing a key technical gate blocking nationwide scaling without dependency on newer hardware. Cybercab production planned for later in 2026 provides future fleet supply.
news.impact_reason: Mildly positive: removes a major technical barrier (software readiness) to scaling, though hardware (Cybercab) production and regulatory constraints remain unresolved bottlenecks.

news.title: Tesla Q1 Earnings Preview Highlights Robotaxi Rollout Widening to Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla is expanding its Robotaxi rollout to Dallas and Houston amid Q1 earnings preparations, alongside a planned capex surge to $20B focused on autonomous capabilities.
news.impact_reason: New city launches in Dallas and Houston directly accelerate unsupervised robotaxi scaling by expanding geofences and active markets in Texas.

news.title: Tesla Q1 2026 Update Details Robotaxi Expansion Groundwork to Additional US Metros
news.summary: Tesla's official Q1 2026 Update states continued progress on Robotaxi service expansion to additional major U.S. metros through testing and permitting activities.
news.impact_reason: Official confirmation of groundwork for new metro expansions supports future scaling velocity by advancing regulatory and testing prerequisites, though lacks specific timelines or fleet sizes.

news.title: Global Robotaxi Market Projected at $168B by 2035 with 3.6M Vehicles
news.summary: Counterpoint Research projects the global robotaxi market will reach $168 billion by 2035 with 3.6 million vehicles deployed, with the US led by Waymo and Tesla while China accelerates deployment. This establishes the competitive and market-structure context for Tesla's scaling strategy.
news.impact_reason: Neutral: macro market analysis without Tesla-specific operational milestones; provides context but does not directly advance or constrain Tesla's fleet deployment velocity.

news.title: Tesla Plans to Double Austin Robotaxi Fleet to ~60 by December
news.summary: Elon Musk announced plans to double the Austin Robotaxi fleet from 29-30 current vehicles to about 60 next month, far short of the prior 500-vehicle end-2025 target. All current Austin rides remain supervised with safety drivers.
news.impact_reason: Promises fleet growth but remains supervised and well below targets, resulting in neutral net effect on unsupervised scaling velocity in Austin.

news.title: Tesla Doubles Austin Robotaxi Geofence to 171 sq mi, Adds Airport
news.summary: Tesla expanded its Austin Robotaxi service area by 86 square miles to 171 square miles total, the third and largest expansion since launch, now including Austin airport and surpassing Waymo's area. This follows prior expansions from 20 to 85 square miles.
news.impact_reason: Doubling the Austin geofence to over 170 square miles in rapid succession significantly increases the operational area for unsupervised rides, enabling more rides and data collection to accelerate fleet scaling.

news.title: Tesla Dallas-Houston Robotaxi Fleet Doubles to Four Vehicles
news.summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla increased its unsupervised fleet from one to two vehicles in both Dallas and Houston within days of launch. Availability remained low at 0-2% over the first 24 hours with brief spikes.
news.impact_reason: Doubling fleet size in new cities shortly after launch indicates early scaling progress, though low availability tempers the velocity impact.

news.title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, adding compact geofence areas in each city alongside ongoing operations in Austin. This triples Tesla's active Texas hubs, building on months of unsupervised rides in Austin.
news.impact_reason: Launching unsupervised service in two major new Texas cities directly triples the state footprint and demonstrates rapid geographic scaling beyond Austin, aligning with H1 2026 multi-city rollout plans.

news.title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
news.summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
news.impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

news.title: TechCrunch: Tesla Brings Robotaxi Service to Dallas and Houston
news.summary: Tesla expanded unsupervised robotaxi operations to Dallas and Houston, now operating in three Texas cities after Austin launch, with video confirmation of no human drivers or monitors.
news.impact_reason: Confirms operational expansion in Texas, concentrating resources and regulatory learnings in a favorable state to boost unsupervised scaling speed across Austin and new areas.
